---
title: "Midterm Test"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
editor: visual
---

## Part A: Data 

```{r}
library(glue)
library(rio)
library(dplyr)
library(remotes)
library(gapr)
library(janitor)
```

```{r}
download_file <- function(year) {
  link <- glue::glue("https://www.oregon.gov/ode/educator-resources/assessment/TestResults20{year}/pagr_schools_ela_raceethnicity_{year-1}{year}.xlsx")
  rio::import(link, setclass = "tibble", na = c("-", "--", "*"))
}
```

1.  

```{r}
all_data <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  download_file(15),
  download_file(16),
  download_file(17),
  download_file(18)
)

#View(all_data)
head(all_data)
```

```{r}
clean_data <- all_data %>%
  dplyr::filter(`Student Group` %in% c("White", "Hispanic/Latino")) %>%
  dplyr::select(
    academic_year = `Academic Year`,
    district = District,
    school = School,
    student_group = `Student Group`,
    grade_level = `Grade Level`,
    `Number Level 1`,
    `Number Level 2`,
    `Number Level 3`,
    `Number Level 4`
  ) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = dplyr::starts_with("Number Level"),
    names_to = "level",
    values_to = "n"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    level = stringr::str_remove(level, "Number Level ")
  ) %>%
  tidyr::drop_na(n)
```

```{r}
names(all_data)

```

```{r}
head(clean_data)
```

2.  

```{r}
d <- clean_data %>%
  dplyr::group_by(
    academic_year,
    district,
    student_group,
    level
  ) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(
    n = sum(n),
    n_schools = dplyr::n_distinct(school),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from = student_group,
    values_from = n
  ) %>%
  janitor::clean_names() %>%
  tidyr::drop_na(hispanic_latino, white)

head(d)
```

# **Part B: Achievement disparities**

```{r}
#install.packages("remotes")
#remotes::install_github("datalorax/gapr")
```

```{r}
d[1:4, ] |> # data source - just first four rows for first district
  estimate_v("white", "hispanic_latino") # columns for reference/focal groups
```

1.  

```{r}
#using group_by() |> nest() |> map()
usinggroup <- d %>%
  dplyr::group_by(academic_year, district) %>%
  tidyr::nest() %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    results = purrr::map(
      data,
      ~ gapr::estimate_v(.x, "white", "hispanic_latino")
    )
  ) %>%
  tidyr::unnest(results)

```

```{r}
#using nest_by() |> summarize()

usingnest <- d %>%
  dplyr::nest_by(academic_year, district) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(
    gapr::estimate_v(data, "white", "hispanic_latino")
  )
```

```{r}
dplyr::left_join(
  usinggroup,
  usingnest,
    by = c("academic_year", "district"),
  suffix = c("_map", "_summarize")
) %>%
  ggplot2::ggplot(
    ggplot2::aes(x = v_summarize, y = v_map)
  ) +
  ggplot2::geom_point(color = "grey70", size = 3) +
  ggplot2::geom_abline(color = "skyblue", linewidth = 1.5) +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal() +
  ggplot2::labs(
    x = "V (`nest_by() |> summarize()`)",
    y = "V (`group_by() |> nest() |> mutate() |> map()`)"
  )
```

```{r}
distsricts <- unique(d$district)[1:100]

plot_data <- usingnest %>%
  dplyr::filter(district %in% distsricts)

one_district <- distsricts[1]
```

```{r}
plot_data %>%
  dplyr::filter(district == one_district) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    direction = dplyr::if_else(
      v < 0,
      "White higher",
      "Hispanic/Latino higher"
    )
  ) %>%
  ggplot2::ggplot(
    ggplot2::aes(x = academic_year, y = v, group = 1)
  ) +
  ggplot2::geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "red") +
  ggplot2::geom_line(color = "gray80") +
  ggplot2::geom_errorbar(
    ggplot2::aes(
      ymin = v - 1.96 * v_se,
      ymax = v + 1.96 * v_se
    ),
    width = 0.2,
    color = "gray50"
  ) +
  ggplot2::geom_point(
    ggplot2::aes(color = direction),
    size = 3
  ) +
  ggplot2::scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "White higher" = "pink",
      "Hispanic/Latino higher" = "blue"
    ),
    guide = "none"
  ) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    title = paste("Achievement Gap Estimates:", one_district),
    subtitle = "Negative values indicate White students have higher average achievement\nPositive values indicate Hispanic/Latino students have higher average achievement",
    x = "School year",
    y = "Average achievement difference (SD units)"
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
districts <- unique(usingnest$district)[1:100]

plot_data <- usingnest %>%
  dplyr::filter(district %in% districts)
```

```{r}
plot_data <- usingnest %>%
  dplyr::filter(district %in% districts) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    direction = dplyr::if_else(
      v < 0,
      "White higher",
      "Hispanic/Latino higher"
    )
  )

plot_data %>%
  dplyr::group_by(district) %>%
  dplyr::group_split() %>%
  purrr::map(
    ~ ggplot2::ggplot(.x, ggplot2::aes(x = academic_year, y = v, group = 1)) +
      ggplot2::geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "red") +
      ggplot2::geom_line(color = "gray80") +
      ggplot2::geom_errorbar(
        ggplot2::aes(
          ymin = v - 1.96 * v_se,
          ymax = v + 1.96 * v_se
        ),
        width = 0.2,
        color = "gray50"
      ) +
      ggplot2::geom_point(
        ggplot2::aes(color = direction),
        size = 3
      ) +
      ggplot2::scale_color_manual(
        values = c(
          "White higher" = "pink",
          "Hispanic/Latino higher" = "blue"
        ),
        guide = "none"
      ) +
      ggplot2::labs(
        title = paste("Achievement Gap Estimates:", unique(.x$district)),
        subtitle = "Negative values indicate White students have higher average achievement\nPositive values indicate Hispanic/Latino students have higher average achievement",
        caption = paste("Note: District included", unique(.x$n_schools), "schools."),
        x = "School year",
        y = "Average achievement difference (SD units)"
      ) +
      ggplot2::theme_minimal()
  )
```

```{r}
plot_data %>%
  dplyr::group_by(district) %>%
  tidyr::nest() %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    plots = purrr::map(
      data,
      ~ ggplot2::ggplot(.x, ggplot2::aes(x = academic_year, y = v, group = 1)) +
        ggplot2::geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "red") +
        ggplot2::geom_line(color = "gray80") +
        ggplot2::geom_errorbar(
          ggplot2::aes(
            ymin = v - 1.96 * v_se,
            ymax = v + 1.96 * v_se
          ),
          width = 0.2,
          color = "gray50"
        ) +
        ggplot2::geom_point(
          ggplot2::aes(color = direction),
          size = 3
        ) +
        ggplot2::scale_color_manual(
          values = c(
            "White higher" = "pink",
            "Hispanic/Latino higher" = "blue"
          ),
          guide = "none"
        ) +
        ggplot2::labs(
          title = paste("Achievement Gap Estimates:", unique(.x$district)),
          subtitle = "Negative values indicate White students have higher average achievement\nPositive values indicate Hispanic/Latino students have higher average achievement",
          caption = paste("Note: District included", unique(.x$n_schools), "schools."),
          x = "School year",
          y = "Average achievement difference (SD units)"
        ) +
        ggplot2::theme_minimal()
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::pull(plots)
```
